title: Smoky Sweet American Corn Skillet
description: A festive American dish featuring charred and bouncy corn, this skillet is perfect for any occasion, made with cornstarch and a tadka for added flavor, and is gluten-free friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the corn kernels and cook until they are lightly charred, stirring occasionally.
In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and chili powder.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the skillet and stir to combine, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
Squeeze the lime juice over the top of the corn mixture and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer the mixture for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the corn is tender and the liquid has thickened.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the smoky sweet corn skillet hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
